Treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) were reported for 105 (59.8%) subjects in the Placebo arm and 76 (80.8%) subjects in the Dupilumab arm. Grade 3–4 TEAEs occurred in 13.4% of Placebo subjects compared with 4.6% in the Dupilumab group. Serious adverse events were reported in 2.4% and 22.3% of subjects, respectively.
Key: TEAE=treatment-emergent adverse event; SAE=serious adverse event. a Adverse events reported as related. b Excludes Grade 5.

Treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) were reported for 77 (42.8%) subjects in the Chemotherapy arm and 50.7% in the Pembrolizumab arm. Grade 3–4 TEAEs occurred in 2.3% of Chemotherapy subjects compared with 11.2% in the Pembrolizumab group. Serious adverse events were reported in 16.2% and 10.5% of subjects, respectively.
